title: Secure system development lifecycle evidence

compliance.assurance_rationale: Auto-migrated from cat-22 markdown on Phase 1.3 run, then normalised in Phase A (clause 'SAMA Cyber Security Framework' → '3.1.1'). Requires SME review to confirm clause accuracy, mode (satisfies vs detects-violation-of), and assurance level (contributing/partial/full).

dataSources: Windows Security Event Logs, network infrastructure logs, cloud audit trails, national cybersecurity platform feeds, financial system logs, data protection compliance records, incident response logs, security assessment evidence
app: Splunk Enterprise Security (263), Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows (742), Splunk ITSI (1841), Splunk Add-on for Palo Alto Networks (2757), Splunk Common Information Model Add-on (1621)
spl:
index=sdlc sourcetype="jira:issue" project="SEC" earliest=-90d
| search labels="*SAMA*" AND status!="Done"
| stats count by assignee, status
value: Aligns SAMA Cyber Security Framework supervisory expectations with measurable Splunk evidence for secure system development lifecycle evidence.
implementation: (1) Normalize bilingual field extractions in props.conf where required. (2) Map IP ranges and business units to national critical infrastructure and banking registers. (3) Route SAMA, QCB, NESA, and PDPL alerts to regional bilingual runbooks. (4) Weekly leadership digest with regulator-specific views.
visualization: Bar chart (sector), Table (top flows), Single value (policy denies).
cimSpl: | tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Network_Traffic.All_Traffic by All_Traffic.action, All_Traffic.src, All_Traffic.dest, All_Traffic.dest_port | sort - count
knownFalsePositives: Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
